mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that lifts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements that accelerate earnings growth and drive positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in post-recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL correlates with positive 20-day forward returns, or the negative relationship vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 250 thousand signal robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of months where PAYEMS exceeds 250, or the threshold effect is not statistically distinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion that reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns remain flat during synchronized expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with elevated inflation during a tightening cycle compress equity multiples and trigger negative short-term market returns as discount rates increase.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive during tightening regimes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent, or the joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns.
